<content>1: // ─── Relay Stack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // The complete relay wiring: OpenCode client, SSE consumer, event translator,
3: // WebSocket handler, session manager, permission/question bridges.
4: //
5: // Extracted from skeleton.ts so integration tests exercise the exact same
6: // wiring as production. skeleton.ts is now a thin CLI wrapper around this.
7: 
8: import { stat } from "node:fs/promises";
9: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
10: import { homedir } from "node:os";
11: import { join, resolve } from "node:path";
12: import { type ClientInitDeps, handleClientConnected } from "./client-init.js";
13: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
14: import { createTranslator } from "./event-translator.js";
15: import { dispatchMessage, type HandlerDeps } from "./handlers/index.js";
16: import { getClientIp, parseCookies } from "./http-utils.js";
17: import { generateSlug } from "./ipc-protocol.js";
18: import { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
19: import { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
20: import { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
21: import { PtyManager, type PtyUpstream } from "./pty-manager.js";
22: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
23: import { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
24: import { RelayServer } from "./server.js";
25: import { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
26: import { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
27: import { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
28: import { wireSSEConsumer } from "./sse-wiring.js";
29: import { ToolContentStore } from "./tool-content-store.js";
30: import type { PartType, ProjectRelayConfig, ToolStatus } from "./types.js";
31: import { WebSocketHandler } from "./ws-handler.js";
32: 
33: // ─── WebSocket library for upstream PTY connections ─────────────────────────
34: const requireWs = createRequire(import.meta.url);
35: const wsLib = requireWs("ws");
36: const WebSocketClass = wsLib.WebSocket as typeof import("ws").WebSocket;
37: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
38: 
39: /** Per-project relay: all relay components attached to a shared server. */
40: export interface ProjectRelay {
41: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
42: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
43: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
44: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
45: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
46: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
47: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
48: 	/** Gracefully stop relay components (SSE + WebSocket). Does NOT stop the HTTP server. */
49: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
50: }
51: 
52: // ─── Full Stack Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
53: 
54: export interface RelayStackConfig {
55: 	port: number;
56: 	host?: string;
57: 	opencodeUrl: string;
58: 	pin?: string;
59: 	projectDir: string;
60: 	slug: string;
61: 	staticDir?: string;
62: 	/** TLS certificate and key for HTTPS mode */
63: 	tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
64: 	/** Session title for the initial session */
65: 	sessionTitle?: string;
66: 	/** Log function — defaults to console.log, pass () => {} to silence */
67: 	log?: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
68: 	/** Optional pre-initialized push notification manager */
69: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
70: }
71: 
72: // ─── Stack ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
73: 
74: export interface RelayStack {
75: 	server: RelayServer;
76: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
77: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
78: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
79: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
80: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
81: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
82: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
83: 
84: 	/** The port the HTTP server is actually listening on (useful when port=0) */
85: 	getPort(): number;
86: 	/** The base URL of the relay server */
87: 	getBaseUrl(): string;
88: 	/** Stop all components */
89: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
90: }
91: 
92: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
93: 
94: /**
95:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
96:  *
97:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
98:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
99:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
100:  *
101:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
102:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
103:  */
104: export async function createProjectRelay(
105: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
106: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
107: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
108: 
109: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
110: 
111: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({
112: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
113: 		directory: config.noServer ? config.projectDir : undefined,
114: 	});
115: 	const translator = createTranslator();
116: 	const permissionBridge = new PermissionBridge();
117: 	const questionBridge = new QuestionBridge();
118: 	const sessionMgr = new SessionManager({ client });
119: 
120: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
121: 	const cacheDir = join(
122: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
123: 		".opencode-relay",
124: 		"sessions",
125: 	);
126: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);
127: 	messageCache.loadFromDisk();
128: 
129: 	const toolContentStore = new ToolContentStore();
130: 
131: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
132: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();
133: 
134: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
135: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
136: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
137: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
138: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
139: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit
140: 	// pty_close (tab X button) or upstream disconnect.
141: 
142: 	const ptyManager = new PtyManager({ log });
143: 
144: 	async function connectPtyUpstream(
145: 		ptyId: string,
146: 		cursor: number = 0,
147: 	): Promise<void> {
148: 		// cursor=0 : replay entire PTY buffer (use for newly-created PTYs so the
149: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
150: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
151: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).
152: 		const httpUrl = new URL(
153: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
154: 			config.opencodeUrl,
155: 		);
156: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
157: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
158: 
159: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
160: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
161: 				headers,
162: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
163: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
164: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
165: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
166: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
167: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
168: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
169: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
170: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
171: 			});
172: 
173: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
174: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
175: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends
176: 			// replay data in the same TCP segment as the upgrade response
177: 			// (Node.js ws processes both synchronously).
178: 			ptyManager.registerSession(ptyId, upstream as unknown as PtyUpstream);
179: 			let connected = false;
180: 
181: 			const connectTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
182: 				// Remove the session on timeout — it was never successfully opened
183: 				ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
184: 				reject(new Error(`Timed out connecting to PTY ${ptyId}`));
185: 			}, 10_000);
186: 
187: 			// Install message handler BEFORE "open" fires.
188: 			upstream.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
189: 				// OpenCode sends cursor metadata as binary: 0x00 + JSON {"cursor":N}.
190: 				// Always convert to Buffer first and check byte 0.
191: 				let buf: Buffer;
192: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
193: 					buf = data;
194: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
195: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
196: 				} else {
197: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
198: 				}
199: 
200: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
201: 				const text = buf.toString();
202: 				if (!text) return;
203: 
204: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
205: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);
206: 
207: 				// Broadcast to all browser clients
208: 				wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "pty_output", ptyId, data: text });
209: 			});
210: 
211: 			upstream.on("close", () => {
212: 				ptyManager.markExited(ptyId, 0);
213: 				// Only broadcast pty_exited if the session is still tracked.
214: 				// closeSession deletes + broadcasts pty_deleted, so we must
215: 				// not also broadcast pty_exited for the same PTY.  Similarly,
216: 				// if connectPtyUpstream failed the session was never stored.
217: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {
218: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
219: 						type: "pty_exited",
220: 						ptyId,
221: 						exitCode: 0,
222: 					});
223: 				}
224: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
225: 			});
226: 
227: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
228: 				if (!connected) {
229: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
230: 					// Remove the failed session
231: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
232: 					reject(err);
233: 				}
234: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream error ${ptyId}: ${err.message}`);
235: 			});
236: 
237: 			upstream.on("open", () => {
238: 				connected = true;
239: 				clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
240: 				resolve();
241: 			});
242: 		});
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	// ── Health check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
246: 
247: 	await client.getHealth();
248: 	log(`   ✓ OpenCode is reachable at ${config.opencodeUrl}`);
249: 
250: 	// ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
251: 
252: 	const sessionId = await sessionMgr.initialize(
253: 		config.sessionTitle ?? "Relay Session",
254: 	);
255: 	log(`   ✓ Using session: ${sessionId}`);
256: 
257: 	// ── WebSocket handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
258: 
259: 	const wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(
260: 		config.noServer ? null : config.httpServer,
261: 		{
262: 			// In noServer mode, the daemon's upgrade handler checks auth before
263: 			// calling handleUpgrade(). Skip verifyClient to avoid double auth.
264: 			verifyClient: config.noServer ? undefined : config.verifyClient,
265: 		},
266: 	);
267: 
268: 	const clientInitDeps: ClientInitDeps = {
269: 		wsHandler,
270: 		client,
271: 		sessionMgr,
272: 		messageCache,
273: 		overrides,
274: 		ptyManager,
275: 		permissionBridge,
276: 		questionBridge,
277: 		log,
278: 	};
279: 
280: 	wsHandler.on("client_connected", ({ clientId }) => {
281: 		log(`   [ws] Client connected: ${clientId}`);
282: 		handleClientConnected(clientInitDeps, clientId).catch((err) =>
283: 			log(
284: 				`   [ws] Client init failed for ${clientId}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
285: 			),
286: 		);
287: 	});
288: 
289: 	wsHandler.on("client_disconnected", ({ clientId }) => {
290: 		log(`   [ws] Client disconnected: ${clientId}`);
291: 	});
292: 
293: 	// ── SSE consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
294: 
295: 	const sseConsumer = new SSEConsumer({
296: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
297: 		authHeaders: client.getAuthHeaders(),
298: 	});
299: 
300: 	// ── Wire session manager → WebSocket ────────────────────────────────────
301: 
302: 	sessionMgr.on("broadcast", (msg) => {
303: 		// Augment session_switched with cached events (combined protocol)
304: 		if (msg.type === "session_switched") {
305: 			const switchId = (msg as { id?: string }).id;
306: 			if (switchId) {
307: 				const events = messageCache.getEvents(switchId);
308: 				const hasChatContent =
309: 					events?.some(
310: 						(e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta",
311: 					) ?? false;
312: 				if (events && hasChatContent) {
313: 					wsHandler.broadcast({ ...msg, events });
314: 					return;
315: 				}
316: 			}
317: 		}
318: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
319: 	});
320: 	sessionMgr.on("session_changed", async ({ sessionId: sid }) => {
321: 		translator.reset();
322: 		// Rebuild translator state from REST API so it knows which parts already
323: 		// exist in the session we're switching to (prevents duplicate tool_start).
324: 		try {
325: 			const messages = await client.getMessages(sid);
326: 			const parts: Array<{
327: 				parts?: Array<{
328: 					id: string;
329: 					type: PartType;
330: 					state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
331: 				}>;
332: 			}> = messages.map((m) => ({
333: 				parts: (m as { parts?: unknown[] }).parts as
334: 					| Array<{
335: 							id: string;
336: 							type: PartType;
337: 							state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
338: 					  }>
339: 					| undefined,
340: 			}));
341: 			translator.rebuildStateFromHistory(parts);
342: 		} catch {
343: 			// Non-fatal — translator will treat all parts as new
344: 		}
345: 	});
346: 
347: 	// ── Handle incoming messages from browser ───────────────────────────────
348: 	// Messages are serialized: each handler completes before the next starts.
349: 	// Without this, concurrent async handlers (e.g. new_session + switch_session)
350: 	// race — createSession's broadcast can arrive on the client AFTER
351: 	// a switch_session reply, clearing messages the user just switched to.
352: 
353: 	const handlerDeps: HandlerDeps = {
354: 		wsHandler,
355: 		client,
356: 		sessionMgr,
357: 		messageCache,
358: 		permissionBridge,
359: 		questionBridge,
360: 		overrides,
361: 		ptyManager,
362: 		toolContentStore,
363: 		config,
364: 		log,
365: 		connectPtyUpstream,
366: 	};
367: 
368: 	let _messageQueue = Promise.resolve();
369: 
370: 	wsHandler.on("message", ({ clientId, handler, payload }) => {
371: 		_messageQueue = _messageQueue.then(async () => {
372: 			try {
373: 				await dispatchMessage(handlerDeps, clientId, handler, payload);
374: 			} catch (err) {
375: 				log(`   [ws] Error handling ${handler}:`, formatErrorDetail(err));
376: 				wsHandler.sendTo(
377: 					clientId,
378: 					RelayError.fromCaught(err, "HANDLER_ERROR").toMessage(),
379: 				);
380: 			}
381: 		});
382: 	});
383: 
384: 	// ── SSE event wiring (translate → filter → cache → broadcast) ──────────
385: 
386: 	wireSSEConsumer(
387: 		{
388: 			translator,
389: 			sessionMgr,
390: 			messageCache,
391: 			permissionBridge,
392: 			questionBridge,
393: 			overrides,
394: 			toolContentStore,
395: 			wsHandler,
396: 			pushManager: config.pushManager,
397: 			log,
398: 		},
399: 		sseConsumer,
400: 	);
401: 
402: 	await sseConsumer.connect();
403: 
404: 	// ── Permission/question timeout checks ──────────────────────────────────
405: 
406: 	const timeoutTimer = setInterval(() => {
407: 		const timedOutPerms = permissionBridge.checkTimeouts();
408: 		for (const id of timedOutPerms) {
409: 			wsHandler.broadcast({
410: 				type: "permission_resolved",
411: 				requestId: id,
412: 				decision: "timeout",
413: 			});
414: 		}
415: 		const timedOutQuestions = questionBridge.checkTimeouts();
416: 		for (const id of timedOutQuestions) {
417: 			questionBridge.remove(id);
418: 			wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "ask_user_resolved", toolId: id });
419: 		}
420: 	}, 30_000);
421: 
422: 	// Don't let the timer keep the process alive
423: 	if (
424: 		timeoutTimer &&
425: 		typeof timeoutTimer === "object" &&
426: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
427: 	) {
428: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
429: 	}
430: 
431: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
432: 
433: 	return {
434: 		wsHandler,
435: 		sseConsumer,
436: 		client,
437: 		sessionMgr,
438: 		translator,
439: 		permissionBridge,
440: 		questionBridge,
441: 
442: 		async stop() {
443: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
444: 			overrides.dispose();
445: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
446: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
447: 			wsHandler.close();
448: 		},
449: 	};
450: }
451: 
452: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
453: 
454: /**
455:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
456:  *
457:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
458:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
459:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
460:  */
461: export async function createRelayStack(
462: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
463: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
464: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
465: 
466: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
467: 
468: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
469: 	if (!pushMgr) {
470: 		try {
471: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
472: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
473: 			await pushMgr.init();
474: 		} catch {
475: 			pushMgr = undefined;
476: 		}
477: 	}
478: 
479: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
480: 
481: 	const server = new RelayServer({
482: 		port: config.port,
483: 		host: config.host,
484: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
485: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
486: 		tls: config.tls,
487: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
488: 	});
489: 
490: 	server.addProject({
491: 		slug: config.slug,
492: 		directory: config.projectDir,
493: 		title: config.slug,
494: 	});
495: 
496: 	await server.start();
497: 
498: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
499: 	if (!maybeServer) {
500: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
501: 	}
502: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
503: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
504: 
505: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
506: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
507: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
508: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
509: 
510: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
511: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
512: 
513: 	const getProjectList = () =>
514: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
515: 			slug: p.slug,
516: 			title: p.title,
517: 			directory: p.directory,
518: 		}));
519: 
520: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
521: 	async function addProjectRelay(
522: 		directory: string,
523: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
524: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
525: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
526: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
527: 		}
528: 		directory = resolve(directory);
529: 
530: 		// Check if directory is already registered
531: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
532: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
533: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
534: 			}
535: 		}
536: 
537: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
538: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
539: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
540: 			throw new Error(
541: 				dirStat
542: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
543: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
544: 			);
545: 		}
546: 
547: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
548: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
549: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
550: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
551: 
552: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
553: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
554: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
555: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
556: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
557: 		}
558: 
559: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
560: 		try {
561: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
562: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
563: 				httpServer,
564: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
565: 				projectDir: directory,
566: 				slug,
567: 				noServer: true,
568: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
569: 				log,
570: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
571: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
572: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
573: 			});
574: 
575: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
576: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
577: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
578: 
579: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
580: 		} catch (err) {
581: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
582: 			relays.delete(slug);
583: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
584: 			throw err;
585: 		} finally {
586: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
587: 		}
588: 
589: 		return { slug, title, directory };
590: 	}
591: 
592: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
593: 
594: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
595: 		httpServer,
596: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
597: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
598: 		slug: config.slug,

2: // Sliding-window rate limiter for per-client message throttling.
3: // Tracks timestamps per client ID and enforces a configurable limit
4: // within a rolling time window.
5: 
6: /** Configuration for RateLimiter. */
7: export interface RateLimiterConfig {
8: 	/** Maximum messages allowed within the window. Default: 5. */
9: 	maxMessages?: number;
10: 	/** Window duration in milliseconds. Default: 10_000 (10s). */
11: 	windowMs?: number;
12: 	/** Injectable clock for testing. Default: Date.now. */
13: 	now?: () => number;
14: }
15: 
16: /** Result from a rate-limit check. */
17: export interface RateLimitResult {
18: 	/** Whether the message is allowed. */
19: 	allowed: boolean;
20: 	/** If rejected, how many ms until the next slot opens. */
21: 	retryAfterMs?: number;
22: }
23: 
24: export class RateLimiter {
25: 	private readonly maxMessages: number;
26: 	private readonly windowMs: number;
27: 	private readonly now: () => number;
28: 
29: 	/** Per-client sliding window: client ID → sorted array of timestamps. */
30: 	private readonly windows = new Map<string, number[]>();
31: 
32: 	constructor(config: RateLimiterConfig = {}) {
33: 		this.maxMessages = config.maxMessages ?? 5;
34: 		this.windowMs = config.windowMs ?? 10_000;
35: 		this.now = config.now ?? Date.now;
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	/**
39: 	 * Check whether a message from `clientId` is allowed.
40: 	 * If under the limit, records the timestamp and returns allowed.
41: 	 * If over the limit, returns the time until the oldest entry expires.
42: 	 */
43: 	check(clientId: string): RateLimitResult {
44: 		const now = this.now();
45: 		const timestamps = this.prune(clientId, now);
46: 
47: 		if (timestamps.length < this.maxMessages) {
48: 			timestamps.push(now);
49: 			return { allowed: true };
50: 		}
51: 
52: 		// Over limit — calculate when the oldest message in the window expires.
53: 		const oldest = timestamps[0]!;
54: 		const retryAfterMs = oldest + this.windowMs - now;
55: 
56: 		return { allowed: false, retryAfterMs };
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	/**
60: 	 * Returns the number of remaining allowed messages for `clientId`
61: 	 * in the current window.
62: 	 */
63: 	remaining(clientId: string): number {
64: 		const now = this.now();
65: 		const timestamps = this.prune(clientId, now);
66: 		return Math.max(0, this.maxMessages - timestamps.length);
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	/**
70: 	 * Remove stale client entries whose timestamps have all expired.
71: 	 * Useful for periodic maintenance to avoid unbounded map growth.
72: 	 */
73: 	cleanup(): void {
74: 		const now = this.now();
75: 		const cutoff = now - this.windowMs;
76: 
77: 		for (const [clientId, timestamps] of this.windows) {
78: 			// Remove expired timestamps
79: 			const fresh = timestamps.filter((t) => t > cutoff);
80: 			if (fresh.length === 0) {
81: 				this.windows.delete(clientId);
82: 			} else {
83: 				this.windows.set(clientId, fresh);
84: 			}
85: 		}
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	/**
89: 	 * Prune expired timestamps for a client and return the active list.
90: 	 * Creates the entry if it doesn't exist.
91: 	 */
92: 	private prune(clientId: string, now: number): number[] {
93: 		const cutoff = now - this.windowMs;
94: 		let timestamps = this.windows.get(clientId);
95: 
96: 		if (!timestamps) {
97: 			timestamps = [];
98: 			this.windows.set(clientId, timestamps);
99: 			return timestamps;
100: 		}
101: 
102: 		// Remove timestamps outside the window
103: 		const firstValid = timestamps.findIndex((t) => t > cutoff);
104: 		if (firstValid === -1) {
105: 			// All expired
106: 			timestamps.length = 0;
107: 		} else if (firstValid > 0) {
108: 			timestamps.splice(0, firstValid);
109: 		}
110: 
111: 		return timestamps;
112: 	}
